root_cause.text: The XSLT component previously allowed the XSLT resource URI to be dynamically overridden at runtime using the `CamelXsltResourceUri` message header, which could allow attackers to load malicious stylesheets if they could control the message headers.
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes the code in `XsltEndpoint.java` that reads the `XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI` header and dynamically calls `findOrCreateEndpoint(getEndpointUri(), newResourceUri)`, marking the constant as deprecated.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The XSLT component previously allowed the XSLT resource URI to be dynamically overridden at runtime using the `CamelXsltResourceUri` message header, which could allow attackers to load malicious stylesheets if they could control the message headers.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch removes the code in `XsltEndpoint.java` that reads the `XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI` header and dynamically calls `findOrCreateEndpoint(getEndpointUri(), newResourceUri)`, marking the constant as deprecated.

intent_record.what: Added a @Deprecated annotation and an explanatory comment to the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI constant.
intent_record.how: Signals to compilers and developers that this constant is obsolete and provides instructions for the new approach.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove dynamic XSLT resource URI resolution from message headers to ensure predictable endpoint execution.
intent_record.what: Removed the conditional block that creates a new endpoint dynamically based on the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header.
intent_record.how: Prevents the system from dynamically loading and executing new XSLT resources specified via message headers during exchange processing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Ignores the test class because the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header is no longer supported.
intent_record.what: Added @Ignore annotation to the test class and removed an empty comment.
intent_record.how: Prevents the test from running, avoiding failures due to an unsupported feature.

grouping_result.intent: Ignores the test class because the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header is no longer supported.
Remove dynamic XSLT resource URI resolution from message headers to ensure predictable endpoint execution.

consistency_review.core_intent: Deprecate the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI constant and ignore related tests as it is no longer supported.

relevance_review.reason: The group deprecates the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI constant and related tests, directly addressing the root cause by removing the vulnerable functionality that allowed dynamic overriding of the XSLT resource URI.
The group directly removes the dynamic XSLT resource URI resolution from message headers, which is the exact mechanism that caused the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line retrieves the dynamic XSLT resource URI from the message header, directly enabling the vulnerability.
This line creates a new endpoint using the potentially attacker-controlled URI from the header.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement retrieves the XSLT resource URI from the message header, allowing attackers to control it and dynamically override the XSLT resource URI at runtime.
This statement creates a new XSLT endpoint using the attacker-controlled URI obtained from the message header, directly executing the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
CAMEL-4689 - Add option to override XSLT stylesheet used via header

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/camel/
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 4abc7be95f5a39c4ab0781da53e89b13fe863990
Author: Jonathan Anstey <janstey@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Nov 16 18:09:22 2011 +0000

    CAMEL-4689 - Add option to override XSLT stylesheet used via header
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/camel/trunk@1202819 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/camel-core/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/xslt/XsltEndpoint.java b/camel-core/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/xslt/XsltEndpoint.java
index a9b9a4a8ac4.
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the exact vulnerable logic by checking the 'XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI' header dynamically ('String newResourceUri = exchange.getIn().getHeader(XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI, String.class);') and using it to load a new endpoint via 'findOrCreateEndpoint(getEndpointUri(), newResourceUri)', allowing an attacker to control the stylesheet URI.

    CAMEL-4689 - Add option to override XSLT stylesheet used via header

index a9b9a4a8ac4.
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the ability to dynamically override the XSLT resource URI using 'exchange.getIn().getHeader(XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI, String.class)', which is the root cause of the vulnerability. There are no restrictions or validations on the newResourceUri, meaning this commit introduces the vulnerable behavior.
